differences.title: 1. CORE TECHNOLOGY
differences.leftDesc: Leverages Vue.js reactivity system for components and state management.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles components to imperative vanilla JS at build time.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: Vue's runtime handles updates and re-renders based on changes.
differences.rightDesc: Svelte detects changes at compile time, no virtual DOM.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Optimized Vue.js runtime performance, especially with Nuxt features.
differences.rightDesc: Smaller bundles, faster initial load due to no runtime overhead.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Familiar for Vue.js developers, slight learning curve for Nuxt concepts.
differences.rightDesc: Simpler syntax, easier to grasp for developers new to Svelte.

differences.title: 5. BUILD TOOLING
differences.leftDesc: Supports Vite and Webpack, offering flexibility in build processes.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily relies on Vite, providing a streamlined experience.

differences.title: 6. SERVER-SIDE RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Mature SSR capabilities with auto-routing and server API routes.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in SSR with a file-system based router and API routes.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Benefits from the extensive Vue.js ecosystem and community support.
differences.rightDesc: Rapidly expanding Svelte ecosystem, gaining traction and tools.
